LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF ARBITRATOR, EACH PARTY MAY SUBMIT TO THE COURT NO LATER THAN MARCH 24 A LIST OF 5 PROPOSED ARBITRATORS ALONG WITH INFORMATION ABOUT THEIR QUALIFICATIONS, BILLING RATES AND WILLINGNESS TO HANDLE THE CASE. THE COURT WILL NOMINATE 5 PERSONS FROM THOSE LIST UNLESS THE PARTIES NOTIFY THE COURT WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THE COURT'S NOMINEES, THAT THEY HAVE AGREED ON AN ARBITRATOR, THE COURT WILL CHOOSE SOMEONE FROM THAT LIST OF NOMINEES. NO OPPOSITION FILED. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

Ntc Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Award Of Arbitration James H Fleming Esq. Rendered On May 4, 2011
Off Calendar Jan-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KEVIN FLAVIT, TEL. NO. 788-7600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL CARROLL'S Petition To Confirm Award Of Arbitrator James H Fleming Esq. Rendered On May 4, 2011 is Granted. The hearing was continued from November 4, 2011 to permit further filings on Mr. Eng's allegations the arbitrator was biased against him. The court has reviewed the declaration of the arbitrator which shall be filed and all papers filed by the parties since the prior hearing. There are no grounds to deny confirmation of the award. The allegations of bias by the arbitrator are unfounded. There is no evidence of bias against Mr. Eng. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Arbitrator
Off Calendar Mar-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL'S Motion For Appointment Of Arbitrator, EACH PARTY MAY SUBMIT TO THE COURT NO LATER THAN MARCH 24 A LIST OF 5 PROPOSED ARBITRATORS ALONG WITH INFORMATION ABOUT THEIR QUALIFICATIONS, BILLING RATES AND WILLINGNESS TO HANDLE THE CASE. THE COURT WILL NOMINATE 5 PERSONS FROM THOSE LIST UNLESS THE PARTIES NOTIFY THE COURT WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THE COURT'S NOMINEES, THAT THEY HAVE AGREED ON AN ARBITRATOR, THE COURT WILL CHOOSE SOMEONE FROM THAT LIST OF NOMINEES. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
